channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: INE Recognized as Enterprise and Small Business Leader in Online Course Providers and Cybersecurity Professional Development
item.description: INE, the leading provider of networking and cybersecurity training and certifications, has been recognized as an enterprise and small business leader in online course providers and cybersecurity professional development.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nvidia's Crucial Earnings Report: What Wall Street Will Be Watching
item.description: Nvidia's fourth-quarter earnings report will be closely watched by investors, analysts, and tech enthusiasts alike. Here are the key points to look out for.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk’s DOGE is draining the life from the once-vaunted U.S. Digital Service
item.description: The United States Digital Service (USDS), the storied group of Silicon Valley types brought together by Obama to bring government services into the 21st century, will likely never be the same after the arrival of Elon Musk’s so-called Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE).
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Rust Web Framework Cot Emerges to Simplify Development
item.description: Emerging web framework Cot promises to simplify Rust language development with an easy-to-use API and built-in ORM, aiming to make apps secure by default.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk push to downsize federal workforce runs into Cabinet roadblock
item.description: Elon Musk's push to reduce the federal workforce is running into headwinds as Senate-confirmed Cabinet and agency officials block his immediate efforts to carry out the Department of Government Efficiency's (DOGE) plans.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, The Hill

item.title: Bitcoin falls below 90K in 3-month low
item.description: Bitcoin fell below $90,000 on Tuesday, marking a three-month low for the popular cryptocurrency. The price of Bitcoin dropped more than 8 percent to $87,262 as of Tuesday afternoon, according to Coinbase. This was the crypto coin's the lowest value since November, when it slid to about $86,128.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: New Relic boosts observability platform with AI intelligence
item.description: New Relic announced updates to its Intelligent Observability Platform this week, which the company says will enable a unified view of system relationships and dependencies and intelligently connect technical systems with business context.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bitcoin slides under $90,000, erasing some of the gains made under Trump
item.description: The price of bitcoin fell below $90,000 and other cryptocurrencies saw large drops Tuesday morning, erasing some of the gains digital assets have made since President Donald Trump took office on a pro-crypto agenda.
item.dc:creator: Alan Suderman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft just gave Edge a great new feature to ensure the browser doesn’t slow down the PC, and it’s tempting me to switch from Google Chrome
item.description: In the Edge vs Chrome battle, Microsoft just landed a telling blow – a nifty new RAM limiter feature.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How to set your preferred web browser on your desktop and mobile devices
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy takes you through the steps to easily set your preferred web browser whether you're using a Mac, Windows PC, iPhone or Android.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sweden is investigating a damaged cable in the Baltic Sea
item.description: STOCKHOLM (AP) — Swedish authorities said on Friday they were investigating a damaged cable that was discovered in the Baltic Sea, the latest in a string of recent incidents of ruptured undersea cables that have heightened fears of Russian sabotage and spying in the region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology
item.description: "Data is getting even more gravity in this space because the more data you have, the more accuracy these parameters get in your model," said Bill Vass. The post How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Copilot for Microsoft 365 explained: GenAI meets Office apps
item.description: Initially called Microsoft 365 Copilot when it launched in November 2023, “Copilot for Microsoft 365” brings a range of generative AI (genAI) features to office productivity apps such as Word, Outlook, Teams and Excel.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: No, Apple and Meta aren’t making humanoid robots
item.description: Tech press-landi and the tech blogosphere were abuzz last week with news that both Apple, the company that makes the iPhone, and Meta, the company that makes everybody on Facebook confront a daily flood of horrible AI-generated slop, are making full-size, humanoid robots.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Humanoid robots bust dance moves alongside humans
item.description: Unitree H1 robots dance at gala, blending AI and tradition. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the convergence of cutting-edge technology and traditional culture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
